nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; shallow reefs at depths of 3–100 m

diet.notes: Feed Spirulina, marine algae, high-quality angelfish preparations with sponge matter, mysis shrimp, and frozen shrimp. Feed at least three times daily. In the wild, consumes sponges, algae, bryozoans, zoantharians, gorgonians, and tunicates.

imageCandidates.notes: Main adult species photo from FishBase summary page; filename Popar_ub.jpg confirms Pomacanthus paru. License not explicitly stated on the summary page.
Photo by LASZLO ILYES from Cleveland, Ohio, USA. Taken underwater at Grote Knip, Curaçao on December 31, 2012. Confirms species identity as Pomacanthus paru. High-quality underwater photograph.
Product hero image from LiveAquaria; large format, clean composition typical of retailer product photography.

summary: A striking large angelfish with dark blue-black body, yellow scale margins, and pale blue face. Semi-aggressive; needs 250+ gal tank with ample live rock. Not reef safe.
careNotes: The French Angelfish (Pomacanthus paru) is one of the larger marine angelfish, reaching up to 41 cm in the wild. It tends to dominate the aquarium and requires a very large tank (250+ gallons / ~946 liters) with large amounts of live rock for hiding and grazing. This species is NOT reef safe — it will nip at sessile invertebrates including soft and stony corals and clam mantles. Feed at least three times daily with a varied diet including Spirulina, marine algae, high-quality angelfish preparations with sponge matter, mysis shrimp, and frozen shrimp. In the wild, juveniles serve as cleaners at cleaning stations. Adults form monogamous, territorial spawning pairs. Temperature discrepancy noted: LiveAquaria lists 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); FishBase reports 22.9–28°C; the conservative intersection (22.2–25.6°C) is applied. FishBase notes a maximum recorded lifespan of 27 years. Temperament noted as territorial/monogamous by FishBase, consistent with semi-aggressive classification.
breedingNotes: In the wild, adults form monogamous territorial spawning pairs. Juveniles act as cleaners at cleaning stations. Captive breeding is rarely documented; breeding difficulty considered advanced. Pelagic spawning in the wild.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (41.1 cm TL vs LiveAquaria's ~38 cm — minor disagreement, used FishBase max), temperature range (22.9–28°C vs LiveAquaria 22.2–25.6°C — minor disagreement, used conservative intersection), native range, taxonomy, lifespan (27 years), and diet composition. FishBase also describes territorial pair-forming behavior consistent with semi-aggressive temperament.

fish.breedingNotes: Monogamous pelagic spawners in the wild; captive breeding rarely achieved. Juveniles function as cleaners at cleaning stations.
